24.3 For the purposes of this term a major change is likely to have a significant effect on 
employees if it results in: 

24.3.1 the termination of the employment of employees; 

24.3.2 a major change in the composition, operation or size of the employer's workforce, or 
to the skills required of employees; or, 

24.3.3 the elimination or diminution of job opportunities, including opportunities for 
promotion opportunity or tenure; or 

24.3.4 the alteration of hours of work; or 

24.3.5 the need to retrain employees or 

24.3.6 the need to relocate employees to another job workplace; or 

24.3.7 the restructuring of jobs.

39.4.2 Employees to whom notification of termination of service is to be given on account of 
the introduction or proposed introduction by the employer of  automation  or other like 
technological changes in the organisation must be given not less than 3 months notice 
of termination . 

39.4.3 Should the employer fail to give notice of termination as required in 39.4.1 or 39.4.2 the 
employer must pay to that employee the ordinary rate of pay for a period being the 
difference between the notice given and that required to be given.
label: employment_conditions

Discipline for underperformance 

3.1 For employees who are paid by piecework rates and engaged in the harvesting of 
mushrooms; 

(a) Upon completion of the mandatory six-month period of probation, PVM has 
benchmark minimum and target picking rate standards which are reflected in 
PVM's Picking Speed Policy for all harvest employees who pick mushrooms 
using  automated  hydraulic picking trolleys; 

(b) The relevant standard is compared to the average picking rate for each week 
over varying periods of consecutive weeks for all harvest employees ; 

(c) Harvest employees are initially employed on a trial and probationary basis with 
two assessment milestones 

(i) an initial two-week employment trial period in which the Harvest 
Manager must determine whether the candidate employee has a 
reasonable prospect of becoming a competent harvester or otherwise.

text:
Redundancy 
 

34.1 The Company agrees that no redundancies will take place during the life of this agreement 
as a result of  Automation  arising from a change of mode.  
 
34.2 An Employee’s employment (covered by this Agreement) will be terminated on the grounds 
of redundancy if the Employee’s job is no longer required to be performed as a result of 
operational requirements including, for example, technological change or restricting, and there 
are no reasonable opportunities for redeployment.  

 
34.3 In circumstances of redundancy, in addition to the period of notice provided for in clause 
13, the Company will make a severance payment calculated as follows:  

 
34.4 Three (3) weeks’ pay for each completed year of continuous service and pro-rata for a part 
year of continuous service.

8.1 This Clause applies if the Company is in the process of making a decision to introduce a:

8.2 In this Clause, a major change is likely to have a significant effect on employees if it results in:  

(a) the termination of the employment of Employees; or  

(b) major change to the composition, operation or size of the Company’s workforce or to the skills 
required of Employees; or  

(c) the elimination or diminution of job opportunities (including opportunities for promotion or 
tenure); or  

(d) the alteration of hours of work; or  

(e) the need to retrain Employees; or
